title: Programmatic unification: methods as configured objects (protocol + Trainer + config-schema)
description: cia unified the CLI surface (Spec dispatch) but not the object model. Each method is still bespoke procedural: fit/score are LOCAL closures inside _run(args), the training loop is hand-rolled 4x (draem/featrecon/triad/train.py), load_split repeats 5x, score_arrays tail 4x, and config IS argparse (no typed schema, no programmatic construction). Goal: a method becomes a configured, importable, callable object satisfying one minimal protocol; the runner is generic; the registry is data; the CLI args derive from the config schema. Enables programmatic use (PatchCoreMethod(cfg, dev).fit('bagel') with no CLI) and one config-truth (code + CLI + mlflow params).
design: TWO clean axes, NOT one god-BaseMethod. (A) AnomalyMethod Protocol (fit/score only, minimal) + typed config dataclasses; CLI args auto-derived from config fields; registry = {name: (MethodClass, CfgClass)} data. (B) one Trainer (compose-in HAS-A) for the epoch/batch/opt loop. BOUNDARIES (honesty): no-train methods (patchcore/btf) skip Trainer, not forced; triad stays a meta-method orchestrator calling Trainer 3x, does NOT collapse to one Method; protocol stays fit/score only, compose helpers by HAS-A not a 12-hook ABC. Sequence: protocol+config foundation (patchcore as template) -> migrate rest -> extract Trainer -> heal train.py split-brain -> triad meta.
close_reason: All 7 subtasks done. AnomalyMethod protocol + cli_config (config->CLI) + method_cli (data-registry); patchcore/btf/fused/draem/featrecon = configured classes; Trainer kills all 4 hand-rolled loops (draem/featrecon/triad/train.py); triad stays a meta-orchestrator; enable_tf32 dedup. title: Stage 2 — Isaac Sim RTX 5090 viability spike (gate for the digital-twin render)
description:
1-day spike to confirm Isaac Sim runs for synthetic-data gen on the RTX 5090 (Blackwell sm_120) before committing Stage 1 to it. If it fights, fall back to BlenderProc MVP.

PINNED WORKING CONFIG (from IsaacLab #1888):
- Isaac Sim 5.1.0 + Isaac Lab, NATIVE WINDOWS (not WSL — WSL wedges on this box; Isaac 5.1 supports native Windows, WSL2 only needed for ROS2 Humble).
- torch 2.7.0 nightly cu128 (pip install --pre torch --index-url https://download.pytorch.org/whl/nightly/cu128); ignore incompatible-version warnings.
- NVIDIA driver ~570.86+ / 580 / 590 (avoid 5.1.0-rc.19 + 580-open = startup crash). Use Interactive Path Tracing (standard render = noisy viewport on Blackwell).

KNOWN BUG TO DODGE (IsaacLab #4951, open Mar 2026): TiledCamera HANGS on RTX 5090 in the omni.replicator tiled-render path. Workaround: use standard Camera (not TiledCamera) — identical RGB, fine for single-scene data-gen. TiledCamera only matters for massively-parallel RL (the B/Isaac-Lab path), not our Replicator label-gen.

SPIKE PASS CRITERIA:
1. Isaac Sim launches native-Windows on the 5090 with pinned config.
2. Replicator renders ONE scene with standard Camera -> emits RGB + depth + semantic-seg passes.
3. PASS -> commit Stage 1 (synth defect engine) to Isaac (USD/Omniverse lane signal + B substrate). FAIL -> BlenderProc MVP now, revisit Isaac when Blackwell bugs settle.

Correct 6.0 API established: rep.functional.create.* (immediate prims), rep.annotators.get(), set_capture_on_play(False). Config matrix all fail: [async ON+step]->empty; [async OFF+timeline.play+step]->'renderer failed to advance to scheduled frame'; [async OFF+step only]->empty. Kernel/RTX/Replicator init fine; the frame render won't fill annotators. NEXT-SESSION HYPOTHESES (focused, not smoke): (1) rep.orchestrator.step(rt_subframes=N, delta_time=..); (2) BasicWriter + rep.orchestrator.run() + app.update() drain-loop (the documented writer path, not annotators); (3) render-time may need the 6.0-TESTED Windows driver 581.42 even though BOOT works on 595.79 (Blackwell render vs boot); (4) pip-package vs full workstation-install difference. title: Adopt sdlc-scaffold @ v0.4.1 on dev + prove zero loss vs main
description:
Runbook to adopt sdlc-scaffold (PRIVATE github.com/dimiturtrz/sdlc-scaffold, MAIN-ONLY now (dev dropped), tag v0.4.1; local ../sdlc-scaffold) on a synth DEV branch, plus the protocol to prove nothing is lost vs main. PREREQ: do the critical re-audit (synthscape-c3u) FIRST and only proceed if it greenlights. The value is the SYNC PIPE (future scaffold changes flow via copier update), not new gates.

=== ADOPT (on dev) ===
1. `git checkout dev` (or a feature branch off dev). Never adopt straight onto main.
2. Ensure git read access to the private scaffold (PAT at D:/work/creds/creds/gitpat.txt, or SSH). copier fetches by tag.
3. Generate INTO the repo root at v0.4.1:
   `uvx copier@9.16.0 copy --vcs-ref v0.4.1 --data packages=core,surfscan --data ship_example=false --data enforce_arch_fitness=true --data enable_import_linter=true --data enable_astgrep=true --data enable_jscpd=true --data enable_beads=true --data enable_class_shape_smells=<match synths current> --data coverage_floor=80 <scaffold-url-or-path> .`
   (set toggles to MATCH synths current gates — synth CI enforces ruff/vulture/import-linter/ast-grep + advisory jscpd; confirm each against synth ci.yml before setting.)
4. Since synth is FLAT-layout: in the generated pyproject [tool.structure] LOCAL-SLOT set `test_layout = "flat"` (avoids the mirror restructure; the gate still enforces every logic module HAS a test, just not the path).
5. copier does a 3-way merge on shared files (pyproject/ci/pre-commit). Resolve so synths bits land in the LOCAL-SLOTs: coverage `omit` list -> coverage-scan slot; vulture domain `ignore_names` -> vulture-scan slot; ruff `extend-exclude` (["sim"]) -> ruff-exclude slot; import-linter contract -> import-contracts slot (scaffold auto-generates "core forbids surfscan" = synths exact contract, so likely no edit). [project] deps (torch etc.) are yours -> keep. Resolve any .rej.
6. `git add -A && git commit`; ensure `.copier-answers.yml` (records _commit: v0.4.1 + answers) is committed -> repo is now copier update-able.

=== PROVE ZERO LOSS vs main (the real gate on this task) ===
A. GATE-SET diff: list every gate synth main CI runs (ruff enforced --select + advisory --statistics + format-check; vulture conf80 enforced + conf60 advisory; import-linter; ast-grep; jscpd; coverage --fail-under=80 + 95 advisory; graph.py --assert). Confirm the adopted dev runs ALL of them. Any gate on main but MISSING on dev = LOSS -> restore.
B. CONFIG diff (each must survive): ruff select (synth == scaffold narrow? if synth ratcheted extra codes, preserve via the ruff_select answer or a local override); coverage `omit` (synths full list present in the slot); vulture domain `ignore_names`; import-linter contract (matches); [tool.structure] thresholds (synths values, if any tuned). 
C. BEHAVIOR deltas that are ADDS not losses (verify they PASS, document as intentional): jscpd flips advisory->ENFORCED (does synths dup pass the threshold?); test-mirror is NEW (with test_layout=flat, every logic module needs a test_<name>.py somewhere under tests/ — run graph --assert; if it flags modules, thats missing tests synth genuinely lacks: backfill or coverage-omit the shells). file_min line-floor (advisory, off).
D. DEVTOOLS convergence: synths devtools/*.py are replaced by scaffolds canonical (a strict superset per the consolidation audit — scaffold has file_min + test-mirror + omit.py synth lacked). Confirm synth had NO local devtools improvement the scaffold lacks (if it did, upstream to scaffold FIRST, re-tag, adopt that).
E. LINE-LEVEL review: `git diff main..dev -- pyproject.toml .github .pre-commit-config.yaml devtools` — every changed/removed line must be either (a) an accepted scaffold portable-superset improvement, or (b) a synth customization preserved in a slot. Nothing synth-specific silently dropped.

=== DONE ===
Adopted on dev; full gate suite GREEN (paste output); .copier-answers.yml committed; a written diff-review (E) showing every main->dev change is accept-or-preserved (zero silent loss); intentional adds (jscpd-enforced, test-mirror) documented + passing. Then PR dev->main. If test-mirror backfill is large or a gate would regress, STOP and record the blocker instead of forcing it.
notes: ADOPTED on dev @ v0.4.2 (429b6ba, PR #44). title: Ruff Phase 5 — advisory tools: vulture (dead fns) + coverage (untested)
description: After ruff. vulture: dead functions, advisory, scoped to pure modules (core/, surfscan/evaluation). coverage: untested surface, advisory. Call out shared blind spot: dead code only a TEST references looks live to both. Advisory-first (|| true), don't gate initially.
acceptance_criteria: vulture + coverage wired advisory in CI; a pass run + findings triaged; merged
notes:
VULTURE + COVERAGE — PROVEN INTEGRATION (ported from cardiac-seg PR #5, 2026-07-08). Lint/scan paths here: core surfscan.

VULTURE (two-tier, in the lint job):
- RED (blocks): `uvx vulture@2.16 --min-confidence 80` — near-certain only (redundant conditions, unreachable, unused locals). min_confidence = how sure vulture is its dead-guess is RIGHT (unused-func=60% since it cannot prove no dynamic/cross-file caller; redundant-if=100%), NOT how-dead. 80 = ~zero FPs, no whitelist, but misses dead FUNCTIONS.
- YELLOW (warns): `--min-confidence 60` piped through sed to `::warning file=,line=::` annotations, in a `continue-on-error: true` step. GitHub has NO native yellow check for run steps; yellow = warning annotations, check stays green. CRITICAL: sed line in a BLOCK SCALAR (`run: |`) — a bare `run:` with the sed's ': ' colon-space breaks YAML parse and the WHOLE workflow silently fails to parse (jobs never run). Validate ci.yml with yaml.safe_load before pushing.
- [tool.vulture]: paths = core surfscan (NOT tests — a prod symbol referenced only by a test surfaces as dead here instead of being masked by test fan-in; the trap that fools BOTH tools). ignore_names=["model_config"] + ignore_decorators=["@model_validator","@field_validator","@field_serializer","@model_serializer","@computed_field"] kill pydantic FPs. exclude same dirs as ruff. Hunt PROD-dead code by scanning WITHOUT tests; with-vs-without diff = the test-only set.

COVERAGE (pytest-cov, two-tier):
- FOLD into ONE run: `pytest tests --cov --cov-report=term-missing` = test gate AND coverage data. Do NOT add a separate coverage job (re-runs the suite = heavier). Then `coverage report --fail-under=80` (RED, blocks) + `coverage report --fail-under=95 || echo ::warning::...` in `continue-on-error: true` + `if: always()` (YELLOW). Tiers: red<80 / yellow 80-95 / green>=95.
- [tool.coverage.run] source=core+surfscan; omit only genuine entry/IO scripts. [tool.coverage.report] exclude_lines drops `if __name__=='__main__':` + protocol stubs. pytest-cov -> dev+all extras; uv lock.

THE HONESTY PRINCIPLE (owner, load-bearing): DO NOT raise coverage by omitting whole modules. Logic/eval MUST be tested (wrong logic = wrong numbers). Think in FUNCTIONS with inputs+outputs, not files: a good separation is easy to test (pure logic split from I/O); a bad one binds logic to file/GPU/mlflow. Where a fn MIXES I/O with computation, EXTRACT the pure input->output core and TEST it with equivalence classes; leave a thin I/O shell. `# pragma: no cover` ONLY for genuine external-dependency lines (GPU/cupy/cucim, mlflow network, plt/pyvista render, real-dataset disk reads) — NEVER to dodge testing pure logic.

TESTS: equivalence-class discipline — partition input space, one rep per class + boundaries; each test docstring names the class; terse asserts. Partial coverage usually = happy-path-only existing tests; ADD the missing branches/classes, don't rewrite.

CI HYGIENE (proven): single trigger `on: push:[main] + pull_request:[main]` (a dev push fires only the PR event -> ONE run not two; pull_request tests the MERGE PREVIEW). `concurrency: {group: ci-${{ github.ref }}, cancel-in-progress: true}`. Bonus: the FIRST pytest-cov run surfaced a real broken test import (stale symbol) failing collection — coverage doubles as an import-health check.

title: [epic] Grow the code-quality gate — extend the ruff ratchet + conventions (from cardiac-seg)
description:
Grow the code-quality gate to match cardiac-seg (PRs #1-4). synthscape ALREADY has the CI structure + an early ratchet: ci.yml lint job runs ruff 0.15.13 check core surfscan --select F,I. This epic EXTENDS that ratchet to the full family set + adopts the conventions. Lint paths: core surfscan.

INTEGRATION PATH (extend the existing ratchet; cardiac-seg-proven order):
Already enforced: F, I. Add each family, one cluster per PR, driving it to 0 by FIXING first (owner: fix-all philosophy), then appending its code to the enforced --select:
  B (bugbear; B009 = getattr-with-constant) -> T201 (no prints, use logging) -> PLR2004 (name magic numbers) -> PLC0415 (imports at top; fix circular imports by EXTRACTION, never lazy imports) -> BLE001 + S110 (no blind except: specific exception or let it crash; know what CAN throw) -> C901 + PLR0912/0915 (complexity: split / strategy) -> PLR0913 (too-many-args: config objects) -> FBT (bools keyword-only) -> RUF100 (no dead noqa).
Keep the two-step CI shape: ENFORCED --select (blocks) + ADVISORY full ruff check . --statistics (|| true, surfaces the remaining families). pyproject [tool.ruff.lint].select = the curated full set; E501 advisory if a dense style makes 120 painful.

CONVENTIONS (owner guidance from the cardiac-seg review threads):
- noqa: bare form only, NO prose reasons. RUF100 enforces that a noqa suppresses a REAL violation.
- Owner is AGAINST COMMENTS: minimal, self-documenting code + names (a comment jammed mid-expression once silently dead-coded a term = real bug).
- getattr/hasattr = smell: direct attribute access; B009 catches the no-default form; with-default + hasattr need review.
- CONFIG POLYMORPHISM (if synthscape has strategy-selecting configs): pydantic DISCRIMINATED UNIONS, each variant owns its params + a virtual build() (cfg.build()), not kind/type dispatch. kind = serialization discriminator only. --set variant shim + _lift backward-compat validator.
- Linter = BUG-FINDER: fix what it surfaces (in cardiac-seg it caught an F821 NameError + a comment-jam that dead-coded a physics term).

ADVISORY tools after ruff (own sub-beads): vulture (dead functions) + coverage (untested), advisory-first, scoped to pure modules. Shared blind spot: a dead product fn only a TEST references looks live to both.

Phased PRs, dev->main, each green. Mirror CLAUDE.md convention edits into AGENTS.md.

title: Deploy design: PatchCore matmul-offload (bank as Conv1x1, argmin/top-k on host)
description: Reformulate the PatchCore bank NN for edge: distance-vs-bank = frozen Conv1x1 (W=-2B, bias=||b||^2) that runs on-NPU, leaving argmin/top-k as a host-side op. A concrete heterogeneous edge architecture (backbone+distance on accelerator, selection on host). No training — it is an ONNX-graph reformulation of the existing bank + coreset+int8+PQ compression (~334KB @ 10k x 256, ~9pp image-AUROC cost). Validate parity vs the fp32 kNN. Sub-task of Stage 3 edge deploy (0sq); hardware-gated for on-device latency, but the reformulation + parity check is doable on CPU/GPU now. Evidence: research/deep_dives/2026-07-08_patchcore_bank_on_npu.md.
